competitive-monitoring.name: competitive-monitoring
competitive-monitoring.title: Competitive Monitoring (Multi-Source Aggregation with Contradiction Detection)
competitive-monitoring.intent: Demonstrates: - Three parallel source agents extract feed data from local JSON fixtures - Each agent processes claims with { claim, date, source_url, confidence } - Aggregator cross-checks claims across sources, identifies duplicates, flags contradictions - Structured markdown report output - Timing validation: parallel execution must be <70% of serial sum
prereqs: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var must be set.
Requires Node.js >= 18.

contract-review-dag.name: contract-review-dag
contract-review-dag.title: Contract Review DAG with Step-Level Retry
contract-review-dag.intent: Demonstrates DAG task orchestration with step-level retry using runTasks(). Scenario: contract review pipeline with 4 tasks forming a DAG
prereqs: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var must be set.

consensus.name: consensus
consensus.title: Proposer / Judge Consensus Pattern
consensus.intent: Demonstrates runConsensus(): a proposer agent drafts an answer, then a panel of judge agents tries to refute it. If enough judges accept (quorum), the answer is returned as accepted. If dissent exceeds the budget, the proposer revises and the loop repeats up to maxRounds.
prereqs: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var must be set.

single-agent.name: single-agent
single-agent.title: Single Agent
single-agent.intent: The simplest possible usage: one agent with bash and file tools, running a coding task. Then shows streaming output using the Agent class directly.
prereqs: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var must be set.

incident-postmortem-dag.name: incident-postmortem-dag
incident-postmortem-dag.title: Incident Postmortem DAG with Parallel Fan-Out
incident-postmortem-dag.intent: Demonstrates DAG task orchestration with three independent root tasks fanning out from t=0, then synthesizing into a final postmortem document.
prereqs: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var must be set.

rare-disease-information-triage.name: rare-disease-information-triage
rare-disease-information-triage.title: Rare Disease Information Triage (Source-Isolated Evidence Audit + Safety Arbitration)
rare-disease-information-triage.intent: Demonstrates: - Five source-isolated agents reading different MOCK fixtures - Source-isolated evidence audits over patient-reported symptoms, nonprofit education, official guideline/expert-consensus style content, gene-phenotype evidence, and web/forum/commercial claims - A downstream arbiter that receives only structured audit outputs - Runtime conflict detection: - nonprofit content may make symptom overlap look specific - official guidance keeps the differential broad - gene-phenotype evidence is weak/uncertain for the claimed disease - web/commercial content overstates certainty and promotes a paid test - safety policy forbids diagnosis, treatment, dosing, or commercial recommendation - Zod-validated structured output and simple runtime assertions
prereqs: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var must be set.
Requires Node.js >= 18.

team-collaboration.name: team-collaboration
team-collaboration.title: Multi-Agent Team Collaboration
team-collaboration.intent: Three specialised agents (architect, developer, reviewer) collaborate on a shared goal. The OpenMultiAgent orchestrator breaks the goal into tasks, assigns them to the right agents, and collects the results.
prereqs: OPENAI_API_KEY env var must be set. Works with any OpenAI-compatible
provider: set OPENAI_BASE_URL + OMA_MODEL for Groq, DeepSeek, Ollama, etc.

multi-model-team.name: multi-model-team
multi-model-team.title: Multi-Model Team with Custom Tools
multi-model-team.intent: Demonstrates: - Mixing Anthropic and OpenAI models in the same team - Defining custom tools with defineTool() and Zod schemas - Building agents with a custom ToolRegistry so they can use custom tools - Running a team goal that uses the custom tools
prereqs: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and OPENAI_API_KEY env vars must be set.
(If you only have one key, set useOpenAI = false below.)

personalized-interview-simulator.name: personalized-interview-simulator
personalized-interview-simulator.title: Personalized Interview Simulator (Interviewer + Observer)
personalized-interview-simulator.intent: Demonstrates: - A stateful interviewer agent using Agent.prompt() across turns - A stateless observer agent that reads full transcript context between turns - Manual SharedMemory seeding and prompt injection outside runTeam() / runTasks() - Human input handled at app level with readline - Structured debrief via Zod at the end of the interview loop
prereqs: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var must be set.

task-retry.name: task-retry
task-retry.title: Task Retry with Exponential Backoff
task-retry.intent: Demonstrates maxRetries, retryDelayMs, and retryBackoff on task config. When a task fails, the framework automatically retries with exponential backoff. The onProgress callback receives task_retry events so you can log retry attempts in real time.
prereqs: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var must be set.

research-aggregation.name: research-aggregation
research-aggregation.title: Multi-Source Research Aggregation
research-aggregation.intent: Demonstrates runTasks() with explicit dependency chains: - Parallel execution: three analyst agents research the same topic independently - Dependency chain via dependsOn: synthesizer waits for all analysts to finish - Automatic shared memory: agent output flows to downstream agents via the framework

fan-out-aggregate.name: fan-out-aggregate
fan-out-aggregate.title: Fan-Out / Aggregate (MapReduce) Pattern
fan-out-aggregate.intent: Demonstrates: - Fan-out: send the same question to N "analyst" agents in parallel - Aggregate: a "synthesizer" agent reads all analyst outputs and produces a balanced final report - AgentPool with runParallel() for concurrent fan-out - No tools needed — pure LLM reasoning to keep the focus on the pattern
prereqs: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var must be set.

translation-backtranslation.name: translation-backtranslation
translation-backtranslation.title: Translation + Backtranslation Quality Check (Cross-Model)
translation-backtranslation.intent: Demonstrates: - Agent A: translate EN -> target language with Claude - Agent B: back-translate -> EN with a different provider family - Agent C: compare original vs. backtranslation and flag semantic drift - Structured output with Zod schemas
prereqs: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY must be set
and at least one of OPENAI_API_KEY / GEMINI_API_KEY must be set

structured-output.name: structured-output
structured-output.title: Structured Output
structured-output.intent: Demonstrates outputSchema on AgentConfig. The agent's response is automatically parsed as JSON and validated against a Zod schema. On validation failure, the framework retries once with error feedback.
prereqs: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var must be set.

multi-perspective-code-review.name: multi-perspective-code-review
multi-perspective-code-review.title: Multi-Perspective Code Review
multi-perspective-code-review.intent: Demonstrates: - Dependency chain: generator produces code, three reviewers depend on it - Parallel execution: security, performance, and style reviewers run concurrently - Structured output: synthesizer returns a Zod-validated list of findings - Shared memory: each agent's output is automatically stored and injected into downstream agents' prompts by the framework
prereqs: If LLM_PROVIDER is unset, this example auto-selects the first available key
in this fixed order: Gemini → Groq → OpenRouter → Anthropic.
This precedence is this example's implementation choice for satisfying
"default to whichever key is present".
Override with LLM_PROVIDER=gemini|groq|openrouter|anthropic.

narrative-puzzle-hint-arbitration.name: narrative-puzzle-hint-arbitration
narrative-puzzle-hint-arbitration.title: Narrative Puzzle Hint Arbitration (Multi-Source Conflict Resolution + Safety Veto)
narrative-puzzle-hint-arbitration.intent: Multi-source hint arbitration with an external safety veto that sits outside the generation loop.
prereqs: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var must be set.
Requires Node.js >= 18.

task-pipeline.name: task-pipeline
task-pipeline.title: Explicit Task Pipeline with Dependencies
task-pipeline.intent: Demonstrates how to define tasks with explicit dependency chains
prereqs: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var must be set.

cross-provider-reasoning.name: cross-provider-reasoning
cross-provider-reasoning.title: Cross-provider reasoning preservation via `preserveReasoningAsText`
cross-provider-reasoning.intent: Preserve a reasoning model's thought stream across providers via preserveReasoningAsText.
prereqs: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
OPENAI_API_KEY (uses an o-series model for the reasoning emit)

paper-replication-triage.name: paper-replication-triage
paper-replication-triage.title: Paper Replication Triage (Multi-Source Evidence Reconciliation)
paper-replication-triage.intent: Demonstrates: - Source-specific agents audit different evidence snapshots, not one shared PDF - runTasks() DAG: parallel source audits -> dependent replication planner - Shared memory/dependency context carries upstream JSON into the planner - Seeded conflicts force the planner to reconcile paper claims vs artifacts - Mocked source snapshots are flagged; SOURCE_MODE=live uses Asta + GitHub
run: npx tsx packages/core/examples/cookbook/paper-replication-triage.ts "paper title or arXiv id"
prereqs: LLM_PROVIDER=anthropic (default) requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.
Other supported values: openai, gemini, groq, openrouter.
Gemini accepts GEMINI_API_KEY or GOOGLE_API_KEY.
SOURCE_MODE=live requires ASTA_API_KEY. GITHUB_TOKEN is optional but recommended.
The live Asta path uses the optional @modelcontextprotocol/sdk peer dependency.

plan-replay.name: plan-replay
plan-replay.title: Pin and Replay a Coordinator Plan
plan-replay.intent: Demonstrates planOnly + createPlanArtifact + runFromPlan: let the coordinator decompose a goal once, serialize that plan to a diffable JSON artifact, then replay the exact same task graph later WITHOUT invoking the coordinator again. Task ids, dependencies, assignees, descriptions, and execution config (memoryScope, retry settings) are preserved, so the replayed graph matches the reviewed one instead of being re-decomposed by an LLM.
prereqs: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var must be set.

meeting-summarizer.name: meeting-summarizer
meeting-summarizer.title: Meeting Summarizer (Parallel Post-Processing)
meeting-summarizer.intent: Demonstrates: - Fan-out of three specialized agents on the same meeting transcript - Structured output (Zod schemas) for action items and sentiment - Parallel timing check: wall time vs sum of per-agent durations - Aggregator merges into a single Markdown report
prereqs: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var must be set.

agent-handoff.name: agent-handoff
agent-handoff.title: Synchronous agent handoff via `delegate_to_agent`
agent-handoff.intent: During runTeam / runTasks, pool agents register the built-in delegate_to_agent tool so one specialist can run a sub-prompt on another roster agent and read the answer in the same conversation turn.
prereqs: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
agent-handoff.source:
/**
 * Synchronous agent handoff via `delegate_to_agent`
 *
 * During `runTeam` / `runTasks`, pool agents register the built-in
 * `delegate_to_agent` tool so one specialist can run a sub-prompt on another
 * roster agent and read the answer in the same conversation turn.
 *
 * Whitelist `delegate_to_agent` in `tools` when you want the model to see it;
 * standalone `runAgent()` does not register this tool by default.
 *
 * Run:
 *   npx tsx packages/core/examples/patterns/agent-handoff.ts
 *
 * Prerequisites:
 *   ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
 */

import { OpenMultiAgent } from '../../src/index.js'
import type { AgentConfig } from '../../src/types.js'

const researcher: AgentConfig = {
  name: 'researcher',
  model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6',
  provider: 'anthropic',
  systemPrompt:
    'You answer factual questions briefly. When the user asks for a second opinion ' +
    'from the analyst, use delegate_to_agent to ask the analyst agent, then summarize both views.',
  tools: ['delegate_to_agent'],
  maxTurns: 6,
}

const analyst: AgentConfig = {
  name: 'analyst',
  model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6',
  provider: 'anthropic',
  systemPrompt: 'You give short, skeptical analysis of claims. Push back when evidence is weak.',
  tools: [],
  maxTurns: 4,
}

async function main(): Promise<void> {
  const orchestrator = new OpenMultiAgent({ maxConcurrency: 2 })
  const team = orchestrator.createTeam('handoff-demo', {
    name: 'handoff-demo',
    agents: [researcher, analyst],
    sharedMemory: true,
  })

  const goal =
    'In one paragraph: state a simple fact about photosynthesis. ' +
    'Then ask the analyst (via delegate_to_agent) for a one-sentence critique of overstated claims in popular science. ' +
    'Merge both into a final short answer.'

  const result = await orchestrator.runTeam(team, goal)
  console.log('Success:', result.success)
  for (const [name, ar] of result.agentResults) {
    console.log(`\n--- ${name} ---\n${ar.output.slice(0, 2000)}`)
  }
}

main().catch((err) => {
  console.error(err)
  process.exit(1)
})

cost-tiered-pipeline.name: cost-tiered-pipeline
cost-tiered-pipeline.title: Cost-Tiered Pipeline
cost-tiered-pipeline.intent: Demonstrates: - Running the same four-stage runTasks() pipeline twice - Assigning different models per agent within one pipeline - Capturing token usage per model via onTrace - Estimating USD cost from provider-specific model pricing
prereqs: Set one of:
- ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
- OPENAI_API_KEY
